seed_instruction: Tell a story about a character lost at sea and their fight for survival.
org_constraint: The story should consist of exactly five paragraphs, with each paragraph containing at least four sentences.
new_constraint: The story must contain no more than 18 sentences.
instruction: Tell a story about a character lost at sea and their fight for survival. The story should consist of exactly five paragraphs, with each paragraph containing at least four sentences. The story must contain no more than 18 sentences.
response:
Captain Elias drifts in a small, battered lifeboat far from the nearest island chain. The ocean around him stretches out endlessly, painted in shades of grey that mirror his own weary spirit. He clutches a rusted compass tightly against his chest as if it were the only thing keeping him tethered to reality. Each wave crashes violently against the wooden hull, threatening to capsize his fragile vessel once more.

Days blur together in a chaotic dance of hunger and exhaustion that tests every limit of his human endurance. He rationed the last of his drinking water, sipping only tiny drops to survive the scorching midday sun. Salinity burns his lips whenever he accidentally spills a few precious drops onto his parched skin. His eyes sting constantly with saltwater tears that offer no relief whatsoever to his deepening despair.

Just when hope seems completely extinguished, a faint glimmer appears on the horizon line far beyond the swell. It is merely a trick of the light or perhaps a distant cloud formation playing cruel jokes upon his mind. Yet Elias cannot deny the surge of adrenaline that spikes through his trembling veins at this sudden sight. He grips the oars with renewed determination, pushing forward despite knowing how unlikely salvation truly feels.

Night falls quickly over the darkened sea, bringing with it a cold chill that penetrates even his thin layers of clothing. The stars above seem indifferent to his plight, watching silently while waves crash rhythmically against his sides. Despite the fear clawing at his throat, Elias whispers prayers aloud into the biting wind that carries them nowhere near any shore. This quiet moment of vulnerability makes him feel smaller yet strangely connected to something vast and ancient.

Dawn finally breaks with a golden hue that illuminates the water ahead and reveals a small cluster of rocks breaking the surface. With one final burst of strength, Elias pulls himself toward these jagged formations that look like salvation from afar. He scrambles up the slick stones and collapses onto solid ground, gasping for air that tastes sweet after days of brine. Although his journey has ended in chaos and struggle, he knows he will never again let fear dictate his actions so completely.<|im_end|>
